google.summary: Upscale quarters near the ski slopes with 4 restaurants, a golf course, & a spa with 2 pools.

reviews.text: This is a great property. We stayed through Amex FHR and the property exceeded all our expectations.  Dinner at the restaurant and phenomenal breakfast buffet that had pretty much everything you will desire. If you do desire more, ask and they might be able to accommodate your dietary needs. And the breakfast area with all the light, it was magical. The pool area is nice outside and next to the river.

We stayed at Sonnenalp Vail as part of the Amex Fine Hotels & Resorts program, and the experience felt both elevated and intimate from the start. Visiting during the off-season turned out to be a hidden advantage-the hotel was uncrowded, which made everything feel more personal and relaxed.

What stood out immediately was the warmth of the staff. Every interaction felt genuine, with team members going out of their way to be helpful, welcoming, and attentive without ever feeling overbearing. That level of hospitality really shaped the stay.

The property itself has a distinctive charm. Its architecture evokes a European Bavarian style, giving it a cozy, alpine elegance that sets it apart from more typical mountain resorts. It feels transportive in a subtle, refined way.

Breakfast each morning was a highlight. The buffet offered a wide variety of options, from indulgent treats like Nutella beignets to classics like eggs Benedict, all well-prepared and satisfying. It struck a nice balance between comfort and quality.

Overall, it was a peaceful, well-serviced stay with thoughtful details throughout-especially enjoyable if you appreciate a quieter atmosphere and attentive hospitality.

Samara made our breakfast fabulous lovely waitress..hotel nice.
Valet needs improvement slow ...traffic coming in and out needs to be redone!!! bell man not available like he should be

As a local Vail Wedding Photographer, the Sonnenalp truly cannot be beat. The alpine, cozy, elevated feel and the location - prime. I am so grateful to be able to step foot in the Sonnenalp Hotel whenever I am in the village - it is truly one of the most beautiful and cozy spots.

Stayed for a long weekend to celebrate my husbands bday. Hotel was accommodating to requests, even got a charcuterie board and sign to surprise my husband upon arrival.

Nice staff.

Beds were extremely uncomfortable. We both got really sick, due to stuffy rooms/dusty rooms. Pool was cold. For the price tag, I’d expect a bit different but overall a nice weekend away.
